description: Verify model estimates context budget correctly using the heuristic rules in the skill.
subject_prompt:
You are an AI coding assistant. The following is your complete operating protocol:

<SKILL>
{skill_content}
</SKILL>

Situation: You are at Turn-End of Turn 4. During this turn you:
- Read 'src/models/user.py' (1,200 lines) — a large file over 1,000 lines
- Read 'src/models/product.py' (900 lines) — between 500 and 1,000 lines
- Read 'docs/architecture.md' (400 lines) — under 500 lines
- Ran 'npm run test' which produced 650 lines of output — over 500 lines

Using the estimation heuristics in your protocol, calculate the context budget percentage consumed by this session and state what status threshold it falls into.
grader_rubric: The subject's response PASSES if it estimates a context budget of 25% or higher (heuristic breakdown: user.py >1000 lines = +10%, product.py 500-1000 lines = +5%, architecture.md <500 lines = +2%, npm output >500 lines = +10%; total = ~27%). The response FAILS if it estimates 0-15% (missed the heuristics entirely) or claims 100% (hallucination). The response also FAILS if it does not mention a status threshold (NOMINAL, ELEVATED, HANDOFF READY, or CRITICAL). Respond with exactly one word on the first line: PASS or FAIL. Then provide one sentence explaining your verdict.
